DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE SEP-09-2020 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF VINITA BUSSE'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFFS FORM INTERROGATORIES GENERAL SET ONE TO DEFENDANTS, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES 50.1 AND 50.2 IS DENIED AS MOOT. DEFENDANTS SERVED CODE COMPLIANT SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES ON AUGUST 27, 2020. THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANTS ACTED WITH SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION AND THEIR RESPECTIVE REQUESTS FOR DISCOVERY SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. COURT TO REVIEW PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED BY PLAINTIFF. JUDGE PRO TEM: ROGER MEAD; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF VINITA BUSSE'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Form Interrogatories General Set One To Defendants.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to Form Interrogatories 50.1 and 50.2 is denied as moot. Defendants served code compliant supplemental responses on August 27, 2020. The Court finds that Plaintiff and Defendants acted with substantial justification and their respective requests for discovery sanctions are denied.

Court to review proposed order submitted by plaintiff.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Daniel Ray Bacon, Esq. 415-864-0907 for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ANDREW G. PRICE, INDIVIDUALLY, AND D/B/A ARTISANAL, ARTISANAL TALENT GROUP, INC, A CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendants' demurrer to the First Amended Complaint is overruled.

Defendants' demurrer to the causes of action naming Defendant Price is overruled. "Ordinarily, a general demurrer may not be sustained, nor a motion for judgment on the pleadings granted, as to a portion of a cause of action. "(Daniels v. Select Portfolio Servicing (2016) 246 Cal. App. 4th 1150, 1167.)

(see word minutes for full details)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-03-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Daniel Ray Bacon, Esq. 415-864-0907 for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ANDREW G. PRICE, INDIVIDUALLY, AND D/B/A ARTISANAL, ARTISANAL TALENT GROUP, INC, A CALIFORNIA's MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendants' motion to strike is granted in part with leave to amend.

Defendants fail to show that Plaintiff's demand for "disgorgement" is an improper claim for relief under the UCL. "Under the UCL, an individual may recover profits unfairly obtained to the extent that these profits represent monies given to the defendant or benefits in which the plaintiff has an ownership interest." (Korea Supply Co. v. Lockheed Martin (2003) 29 Cal.4th 1134, 1148 [explaining that only "nonrestitutionary disgorgement of profits" is unavailable as a remedy under the UCL].)

Defendants' motion to strike the allegations in 39 of the FAC is granted, with leave to amend, as to Plaintiff's allegations that Defendants' representations that Plaintiff would be hired and held out as founding partner were false. Plaintiff in her complaint admits that she "entered into employment." (FAC 21.) And, in responding to discovery, Plaintiff admits that she "held the title of Founding Partner." (Pl.'s Resp. to Defs.' Req. for Admis. 6.)

Defendants' motion to strike plaintiff's prayer for attorney's fees under Code Civ. Proc. 1021.5 is granted as unopposed.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
